mentions.note: Destroyer of software valuations through aggressive press releases claiming disruption capability. Taking down IBM (13% loss), Workday, entire enterprise software sector through fear narrative alone.

Has radar issue but company well-run and beloved. Cramer known for backing since early days. Data-center buildout tailwind supports energy stocks. Fundamentals not questioned despite problem; won't back away.

Fundamentals 'real and spectacular' but stock volatile. Q4 beat: $0.45 EPS vs. $0.31E; FY2026: $3.1–3.3B revenue vs. $2.2–2.55B consensus, $1.33–1.48 EPS vs. $1.11E. Real partnerships: AEP $2.65B order, Oracle, Brookfield. Trading 118x 2026E earnings. Wait for 20% pullback; dropped 50% in Dec 2025.
Heavy equipment; on Cramer's list of companies that make things people understand and want; tangible asset beneficiary of software rotation.
Consumer staple; real product; on Cramer's list of in-demand businesses.
Value retailer making things cheaper than competitors; strong business model; beneficiary of rotation to tangible companies.
Value-oriented discount retailer; tangible product focus; attracting rotation from software.
Value retailer; real products; on Cramer's list of understandable, in-demand companies.

Turnaround story: up 137% past 12-months, 17% YTD. Six blockbusters: Cordelia Quattro (parasiticides, 30% growth, market leader), Zenrelli (dermatology, 40% Brazil share), pain, farm brands. Driven by pet humanization (70% puppies outside US, 40% subscription), protein revolution, farm efficiency. Management target 2028: mid-single-digit revenue, high single-digit EBITDA, low double-digit EPS growth.
Logistics company moving things; real business; on Cramer's list of understandable, in-demand companies.
Transitioned from B2C to B2B AI power-generation play on Encon acquisition; up 72% YTD from $167 to $235; CEO solid; $500M buyback announced. Own it, don't trade it.
Consumer staple; real product; tangible business that people understand.
Lost 13% (worst decline in ages) on Anthropic press release. Hard-to-understand business makes it vulnerable to disruption narratives. Fundamentals solid but sentiment destroyed.
Not a great quarter; Anthropic threat narrative making investors nervous; consulting/analyst firms are easy AI disruption targets; say no.
Consumer staple; real products; on Cramer's list of understandable, in-demand companies.
Accounting issues trigger penalty-box status; too early to recommend; need to see next quarter's results.
Semiconductor capital-equipment maker supplying machines to boost memory/storage production; only handful of suppliers; fab lead times ensure years of shortage.
Preferred over Novo Nordisk because Eli Lilly makes great drugs; growth story more attractive than Novo's price-cutting defense.
Semiconductor capital equipment; machines take years to build; supply bottleneck will persist for years; high visibility on memory expansion constraints.
Memory shortage most severe in Cramer's career; customers rationing product with no demand destruction possible; prices can skyrocket indefinitely on AI data-processing needs.
CEO Jensen Wong only person who foresaw memory shortage; reports tomorrow; positioned to benefit from structural shortage as AI demand is insatiable.
Good at price-cutting but not as attractive as Eli Lilly which is good at drug-making; relative to LLY, avoid.
Consumer staple; company that makes products people understand; beneficiary of rotation from incomprehensible software.
Consumer staple making household products; good company on list of understandable businesses.
Data-center infrastructure play up 214% from $172 entry 9 months ago. Recommendation: take 1/3 profits off, let 2/3 ride on house money. Stock extended but core position justified.

Federal-chartered bank with fortress fundamentals: Q4 showed 37% revenue growth, 160% earnings growth, 35% membership growth to 13.7M. FY2026 guidance: 30% member growth, 52% EBITDA growth, 54% EPS growth. Trading at 31x 2026E with PEG of 6.0—reasonable for 50%+ growth. AI cannot disrupt banking.
Storage shortage play with inelastic demand; AI data processing needs create permanent supply constraint; no demand destruction possible.
Testing equipment for memory and storage; only handful of suppliers; shortage extends across entire supply chain, benefiting downstream equipment vendors.
Value-oriented apparel/home goods retailer; reports tomorrow; tangible product focus; attractive rotation candidate.
Relief rally today but reported disappointing numbers after close; sell bounces as temporary. Enterprise software under structural attack from AI platforms writing code better and cheaper.
Cramer listed 'SanDisk' as separate company; now a WDC brand division (acquired 2016). Memory/storage shortage play; customers have no choice but to pay up; orders carefully allocated.
Value-oriented retailer; makes things cheaper than competitors; reports tomorrow; on Cramer's list of understandable businesses.
